cases.title: Clef des Champs
cases.intro: A farm-to-table identity rooted in soil, season, and ceremony — built to feel as alive as the produce it represents.

sections.heading: An identity that tastes like the land
sections.body: We were asked to build a brand that could hold both heritage and modernity — a system that feels handmade yet scales across packaging, signage, and digital. The work began in the fields and ended in a flexible visual language.

items.heading: Approach
items.body: Strategy first. We mapped the rituals of harvest into a modular grid that flexes from a jar label to a billboard.

items.heading: Outcome
items.body: A 30% lift in shelf recognition and a system the in-house team can extend without us.

cases.title: Lumara Hotels
cases.intro: A luxury hospitality identity designed to feel quiet, warm, and unmistakably modern.

sections.heading: Understated luxury, end to end
sections.body: From wayfinding to the booking flow, every touchpoint was tuned for calm. The wordmark sets the tone — a slow, confident italic that reads as effortless.
